chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. No. D-3244 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________ 1) For hearing of CMA No.13996/2025. 2) For hearing of Main Case. 12.08.2025. None present for the Petitioner. Syed Ahsan Ali Shah, Advocate for Respondent/FBR. Mr. Muhammad Ishaq, Assistant Attorney General. ________________ This petition was filed when sales tax registration of the petitioner was suspended by the Assessing Officer through Order-in-Original, which was further confirmed by the Commissioner Inland Revenue (Appeals). On 09.07.2025 the operation of the suspension order by FBR was suspended by this Court and sales tax registration of the petitioner was restored. Learned counsel for FBR submits that in compliance of the order the sales tax registration has been restored and currently there is no proceeding pending for blacklisting the petitioner. He submits that as and when such proceedings are initiated, due notice will be given to the petitioner and it will be heard. The petition in light of the statement of learned counsel for the FBR is disposed of accordingly with consent along with pending application(s). J U D G E J U D G E Nasir/

The petition in light of the statement of learned counsel for the FBR is disposed of accordingly with consent along with pending application(s). J U D G E J U D G E Nasir/ ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. No. D-2962 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________ 1) For hearing of CMA No.13079/2025. 2) For hearing of Main Case. 12.08.2025. Mr. Ahmed Ali Hussain, Advocate for the Petitioner. Syed Ahsan Ali Shah, Advocate for Respondent/FBR. Mr. Muhammad Ishaq, Assistant Attorney General. ________________ This petition was filed when sales tax registration of the petitioner was suspended by the Assessing Officer through Order-in-Original, which was further confirmed by the Commissioner Inland Revenue (Appeals). On 09.07.2025 the operation of the suspension order passed by the FBR officials was suspended by this Court and sales tax registration was restored. Learned counsel for FBR submits that in compliance of the order the sales tax registration has been restored and currently there is no proceeding pending for blacklisting the petitioner. He submits that as and when such proceedings are initiated, due notice will be given to the petitioner. The petitioner is satisfied with the above statement. The petition in light of the statement of learned counsel for the FBR is disposed of accordingly with consent along with pending application(s). J U D G E J U D G E Nasir/
